{"success": true, "name": "nextjs-pages-to-app-router-migration", "description": "Migrate a Next.js Pages Router site (JSON-driven, multi-locale, section components with inline styles) to App Router with Tailwind v4, PPR, code-split sections, locale middleware, and hreflang SEO.", "tags": [], "related_skills": [], "content": "--- name: nextjs-pages-to-app-router-migration description: Migrate a Next.js Pages Router site (JSON-driven, multi-locale, section components with inline styles) to App Router with Tailwind v4, PPR, code-split sections, locale middleware, and hreflang SEO. --- # Next.js Pages → App Router Migration For sites with JSON-driven content, multi-locale support, and many section components with inline styles. The target architecture is App Router + Tailwind v4 + PPR + code-split sections. ## When to Use - Site uses `pages/[[...slug]].tsx` or `pages/[slug].tsx` - Content comes from JSON files, not CMS (loadPageData reads filesystem) - Multiple locales (es/en/nl/de etc.) - Section components with inline styles and `import { theme }` from a shared theme.ts - Components use `useRouter` from `next/router` for locale extraction ## Phase 1: App Router Foundation ### 1. Filesystem Hazard The `[locale]` directory name uses square brackets which are fragile. Create directories explicitly via terminal: ```bash mkdir -p src/app/[locale]/[slug] mkdir -p src/app/[locale]/blog/[slug] ``` Do NOT rely on write_file to create bracketed directories. If write_file targets `src/app/[locale]/page.tsx` and the directory doesn't exist, the file lands at `src/app/` root instead. Verify files exist after every write_file call. If `[locale]` vanishes mid-session, recreate with mkdir then rewrite all page files. Next.js's file watcher can trigger cleanup of unmatched bracket dirs. ### 2. Remove Old Pages Router ```bash rm -rf src/pages ``` If both `src/pages/` and `src/app/` exist, Next.js builds `pages/` routes and ignores `app/`. Check after every build with `find src/app -name '*.tsx'`. ### 3. Tailwind v4 Setup Tailwind 4 uses CSS-first config. No tailwind.config.js: ```css @import "tailwindcss"; @theme { --color-primary: #1B2A4A; --color-accent: #C9A96E; } ``` Classes: `text-primary`, `bg-accent`, `rounded-lg`, `bg-surface-alt`, `text-text-muted`. ### 4. Locale Middleware ```typescript const LOCALES = ['es', 'en', 'nl', 'de'] export function middleware(request: NextRequest) { // URL prefix > cookie > Accept-Language > default // Redirect /path -> /{locale}/path } export const config = { matcher: ['/((?!_next|api|images|fonts|favicon).*)'] } ``` ### 5. SectionsRenderer (Registry Pattern) ```typescript 'use client' import dynamic from 'next/dynamic' const SECTIONS: Record = { hero: dynamic(() => import('./sections').then(m => ({ default: m.HeroSection })), { ssr: true }), // ... all section registrations } // GenericSection fallback for unregistered section types ``` ### 6. Server-Only Data Loading ```typescript import { readFileSync } from 'fs' const REPO = process.cwd() export function loadPageData(locale: string, slug: string) { const content = JSON.parse(readFileSync(join(REPO, 'content', `${locale}.json`), 'utf-8')) } // MUST NOT import from client components. No React imports. ``` ### 7. SEO: hreflang + sitemap Page metadata: `alternates: { languages: { es: '/es/slug' } }` Sitemap generates per-locale entries with alternates. ## Phase 2: Tailwind Migration ### Strategy 1. Create analysis script detecting `import { theme }` and `style={{}}` props 2. Map JS theme -> Tailwind classes (see `references/theme-mapping.md`) 3. Migrate order: Hero -> CTA -> Testimonials -> Blog -> bulk 4. Remove `import { theme }` and `useRouter` from each migrated component ### Component Changes Required 1. Add `'use client'` to components using `useState`, `useEffect` 2. Remove `import { theme } from '../theme'` 3. Replace `useRouter` locale extraction -> locale prop 4. Replace `style={{}}` with Tailwind classes 5. Use `usePathname` from `next/navigation` if router info needed 6. Components that used `useRouter().asPath.split('/')` for locale -> receive locale as prop ### Key Theme -> Tailwind Mapping | JS Theme | Tailwind | CSS Value | |----------|----------|-----------| | c.primary | text-primary / bg-primary | #1B2A4A | | c.accent | text-accent / bg-accent | #C9A96E | | c.bgLight | bg-surface-alt | #F5F5F0 | | c.text | text-text | #444 | | c.textMuted | text-text-muted | #5C6B7A | | c.border | border-border | #E0E0E0 | | r.sm (8) | rounded-sm | 8px | | r.md (12) | rounded-lg | 12px | | r.lg (24) | rounded-2xl | 24px | | s.section (96) | py-24 | 96px | | s.padding (36) | p-8 | 32px | | sz.maxWidth (1200) | max-w-6xl | 1152px | ## Phase 3: Partial Pre-Rendering (PPR) PPR is Next.js 16's built-in static shell + dynamic streaming. Enable with one flag: ```js // next.config.js const nextConfig = { cacheComponents: true, } ``` ### PPR Build Output ``` (Partial Prerender) -- static HTML with dynamic server-streamed content (SSG) -- fully static (Dynamic) -- server-rendered on demand ``` ### PPR Rules 1. **Every uncached data access MUST be inside ``**. ALL filesystem reads, DB queries, fetch() calls must live in a component wrapped in Suspense: ```tsx async function PageContent({ locale }: { locale: string }) { const data = loadPageData(locale, 'home') if (!data) return
Not found
return } export default async function Page({ params }: Props) { const { locale } = await params return ( }> ) } ``` 2. **All route segment configs are FORBIDDEN**. Remove ALL of these: - `export const revalidate = 3600` - `export const dynamicParams = true` - `export const dynamic = 'force-static'` - Any other `export const [configName]` on page files - PPR manages caching itself. 3. **`generateStaticParams` is REQUIRED** for every dynamic route. Must return at least one result. Read filesystem at build time: ```typescript export function generateStaticParams() { const pages = readdirSync(join(process.cwd(), 'nexa-pages')) .filter((f) => f.endsWith('.json')) .map((f) => f.replace('.json', '')) return pages.flatMap(slug => ['es','en','nl','de'].map(locale => ({ locale, slug }))) } ``` 4. **`generateMetadata` must NOT access uncached data** (no filesystem reads). For dynamic routes return minimal metadata; the layout title will serve as fallback: ```typescript export function generateMetadata({ params }: Props): Metadata { return { title: 'Site Name' } } ``` 5. **`new Date()` in server components** counts as uncached data. Hardcode static values. `2026` not `String(new Date().getFullYear())`. 6. **`useRouter` from `next/router` breaks PPR prerendering**. Replace with locale prop or usePathname. 7. **Blog post data format**: `posts.json` may be `{ "posts": [...] }` not a flat array. Always: ```typescript const posts = JSON.parse(readFileSync(path, 'utf-8')) const list = posts.posts || posts ``` 8. **Add skeleton fallbacks per route type** for good UX during streaming. Match skeleton structure to actual page layout. ## Phase 4: Clean Architecture 1. Every section type MUST have a registered component in the SECTIONS registry 2. Replace GenericSection fallback with dedicated generic components (text, card, image, CTA) 3. Add typed SectionComponentProps with correct locale, pageContent, images types ## Pitfalls - **pages/ re-creation**: write_file can silently recreate src/pages/. Check with `find src/app -name '*.tsx'` after every build. - **[locale] directory loss**: Square-bracket directories are fragile. Create via explicit mkdir, verify after every write_file call. - **TypeScript node_modules errors**: React 19 + @types/react mismatches are usually cosmetic. Fix by aligning: `npm install react@19.2.0 react-dom@19.2.0`. - **readFileSync in App Router**: works in server components. `process.cwd()` = project root. Add explicit type annotations for strict .filter() callbacks. - **PPR + empty generateStaticParams**: Returns `EmptyGenerateStaticParamsError`. Must return 1+ result. - **PPR + generateMetadata**: Must be synchronous. Cannot access filesystem/DB. - **Next.js 16 middleware deprecation**: `middleware.ts` still works but warns. Use `proxy` in next.config instead. - **Compose deploy: Traefik labels at service-level**: Must be under `deploy.labels`, not top-level `service.labels`. Docker Swarm ignores service.labels. - **Blog post JSON structure**: `posts.json` may be `{ "posts": [...] }` not a flat array. Always access via `list = data.posts || data`. - **`new Date()` in Server Components with PPR**: Counts as uncached data. Hardcode static values (e.g. `'2026'` not `new Date().getFullYear()`). - **PPR + generateStaticParams must return 1+ results**: If no blog posts exist, still return at least one valid param or the build crashes with `EmptyGenerateStaticParamsError`. - **`generateMetadata` for dynamic routes with PPR**: Must be synchronous and cannot read filesystem/DB. For blog/slug pages where metadata depends on the content, return static fallback metadata and let the layout title serve. - **Section-extra components may use `useRouter` from `next/router` for locale**: This crashes PPR prerendering. Replace with locale prop. The SectionsRenderer already passes `locale={locale}` — use it. ", "path": "software-development/nextjs-pages-to-app-router-migration/SKILL.md", "skill_dir": "/root/.hermes/skills/software-development/nextjs-pages-to-app-router-migration", "linked_files": {"references": ["references/theme-mapping.md", "references/tailwind-migration-patterns.md", "references/ppr-debugging.md"]}, "usage_hint": "To view linked files, call skill_view(name, file_path) where file_path is e.g. 'references/api.md' or 'assets/config.yaml'", "required_environment_variables": [], "required_commands": [], "missing_required_environment_variables": [], "missing_credential_files": [], "missing_required_commands": [], "setup_needed": false, "setup_skipped": false, "readiness_status": "available"}

{"success": true, "name": "org-intelligence-gathering", "description": "Collect all available intelligence on a person/entity across the Ai-Whisperers org — GitHub repos, session history, on-disk data, and external sources. Structured triage from known data to deep-dive.", "tags": [], "related_skills": [], "content": "---
name: org-intelligence-gathering
title: Org Intelligence Gathering
description: Collect all available intelligence on a person/entity across the Ai-Whisperers org — GitHub repos, session history, on-disk data, and external sources. Structured triage from known data to deep-dive.
---

# Org Intelligence Gathering

Systematically collect everything known about a person or entity across the Ai-Whisperers / IvanWeissVanDerPol org repositories, session history, local filesystem, and external search.

## When to Use

- User asks "find everything about [person name]" or "analyze all we have about [entity]"
- User references someone and you need full context — relationship history, repos, docs, session notes
- Preparing for a client meeting, negotiation, or relationship conversation where background matters

## Triage Order (Fast → Deep)

### Phase 1: Session History (fastest yield)
Search for the person across past conversations. Use simple name and partial variations.

```
session_search(query="Sarah Roig")
session_search(query="firstname OR lastname")
```

### Phase 2: GitHub Repo Discovery
List ALL repos in both orgs, filter by name relevance:

```
gh repo list IvanWeissVanDerPol --limit 100 --json name,description,isPrivate,updatedAt
gh repo list Ai-Whisperers --limit 100 --json name,description,isPrivate,updatedAt
# Grep for the person's name or related terms
```

For each matching repo:
1. `gh repo view <owner>/<repo>` — get metadata
2. Clone or read README.md for description
3. Check for `contacts/`, `profiles/`, or person-named directories

### Phase 3: Local Filesystem Search
```
find /root -name "*[Name]*" -type f 2>/dev/null
rg -l "Person Name" /root --type md --type json
```

### Phase 4: Reference Documents (if repo found)
If a dedicated repo exists for the person (like `sarah-roig` or `sarah-therapy`):
1. Read `README.md` for repo map
2. Read `SOURCE_OF_TRUTH/` or equivalent consolidated profile
3. Read treatment plans, clinical summaries, relationship analyses
4. Read contact files (`contacts/`) for business context

### Phase 5: External Search (if needed)
- DuckDuckGo / web search for public footprint
- Check for namesakes that create search noise
- Identify any public controversies or legal context

## Pitfalls

- **Don't skip Phase 1** — session history often has the most context in the shortest time.
- **Private repos need auth** — use GH_TOKEN with adequate scopes. If clone fails, read via gh CLI instead.
- **Namesakes are common** — "Sarah Roig" has a Paraguayan voice actress with the same name. Verify you have the right person.
- **Repo descriptions lie** — a repo called `sarah-roig` with a vague description may contain extremely sensitive data. Handle with confidentiality.
- **Large repos** — if a repo has 3000+ files, don't try to read everything. Read the structural files (README, SOURCE_OF_TRUTH, MASTER_PROFILE).
- **Session search has limits** — search across the last 5 sessions. Use variations of the name if the first pass yields nothing.

## Expected Output

A structured summary covering:
1. **Identity** — name, age, profession, location, living situation
2. **Relationship to Ivan/org** — how they connect, business vs personal
3. **Psychological profile** (if applicable) — attachment style, core wounds, defense mechanisms
4. **Relationship history** — past and current relationship dynamics
5. **Artistic/creative output** — comedy sets, writing, music
6. **Current plans** — travel, work, education
7. **Repositories found** — names, visibility, contents
8. **Any gaps or unknown areas

## Verification

After delivering the summary, check: did you find contact info (WhatsApp, email)? If so, save to memory so you can interact directly with the person if asked in future sessions. This was a key learning from the Superspuma/Sarah case.

# Paraguay Market Research Skill (v2.0)

Manual deep-dive market research for any business vertical in Paraguay.
Use this when search engines block automated queries (Google CAPTCHA, DuckDuckGo
bot detection, Cloudflare). Combines multiple free data sources into structured
lead lists with phone numbers, addresses, and website status.

## When to Use This Skill

- User asks to research a market, find clients, analyze competition
- User wants "all companies" or "all businesses" in a sector
- User wants to understand "who builds" or "who buys" something in Paraguay
- User asks for reseller / distribution / channel opportunities
- Any request involving `.com.py` domains or Paraguayan construction/glass industry

## Execution Pipeline (strict order)

### Phase 0: Understand Scope (first, always)

Before any data collection, clarify:
- What exactly is being sold? (product / service / material)
- Who is the end client? (construction companies? glass shops? hardware stores?)
- Are they looking for DIRECT customers or CHANNEL PARTNERS (resellers)?
- Should the output be a git repo or just a file?
- What tier structure to use for leads? (buyers vs influencers vs distributors)

This prevents wasted effort collecting the wrong type of lead.

### Phase 1: Landscape Assessment

1. **Wikipedia API** (always available, no bot blocking):
   - `curl -sL 'https://en.wikipedia.org/w/api.php?action=parse&page=<PAGE>&format=json&prop=text&origin=*'`
   - Extract tables: find `<table[^>]*class=.wikitable[^>]*>` then parse `<tr>` + `<td>` with regex
   - Best for: building/construction lists, company lists, industry overviews
   - Example: `List_of_tallest_buildings_in_Paraguay` returns all 27 towers with heights, floors, years

2. **DNS reconnaissance** (batch check 30+ domains at once):
   ```python
   import subprocess
   for domain in domains:
       r = subprocess.run(["host", domain], capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=5)
       ips = [line for line in r.stdout.split('\n') if 'has address' in line]
       status = "OK: "+ips[0].split()[-1] if ips else "NXDOMAIN"
   ```
   - Check BOTH `.com.py` and `.com` variants
   - Batch 20-30 at a time, write to execute_code
   - 80-90% of .com.py domains will NOT resolve — that's normal for PY
   - DNS = NXDOMAIN means no hosted website exists (some have Facebook/Instagram only)
   - DNS = OK means there IS a website to visit for contact info

3. **Company website visits** (for DNS-resolved domains):
   - Visit each via browser_navigate
   - Extract: phone, email, address, services/products, key personnel, contact form
   - Look in footer, "Contacto" page, "Nosotros" page
   - Check for WhatsApp link — many PY businesses use it as primary contact
   - If SSL error, try `http://` (non-SSL) or just note the cert issue

### Phase 2: Deep Discovery (Google Maps Method)

4. **Google Maps search** (the goldmine when all search engines are blocked):

   Navigate to: `https://www.google.com/maps/search/<query>`
   Then use `browser_snapshot` to read the results panel from the DOM.

   **Critical: The snapshot IS the data** — the Canvas map element is visual-only.
   All text (business names, phones, addresses, ratings, website links) is in the
   `<feed>` element's `<article>` children in the DOM.

   **Query strategy** — run 3-5 different queries per vertical. Each query returns
   ~7-8 results. Different queries = different sets of companies:
   - `[category] Asuncion Paraguay` — general
   - `[product/detail] Asuncion Paraguay` — specific
   - `[service type] Asuncion Paraguay` — service-focused

   **To run a new search**: Find the combobox in the snapshot (it shows the current
   search text), use `browser_type` with the element ref to type the new query,
   then `browser_press` with key="Enter", then `browser_snapshot` to get results.

   **Data extraction from snapshot**: Each `<article>` contains:
   - Business name (in a `<link>` element)
   - Star rating (as an image, check ref=e*'s alt text like "4,5 estrelas")
   - Address / description text
   - Phone number (typically in a generic/StaticText block like "Fechado · Abre ... +595 XXX XXX XXX")
   - Website link (may say "Acessar o site de [Company]")
   - Routing button

   **Important: Each query returns DIFFERENT companies.** Results are limited to ~7-8
   per query. Running the same query again gets the same results. To find more
   companies, run MORE queries with different search terms — not the same one repeatedly.

   **Query templates by vertical**:

   | Vertical | Queries to try |
   |----------|---------------|
   | Construction | `constructora civil Asuncion Paraguay`, `constructora edificio Asuncion`, `constructora torre Asuncion` |
   | Glass/Facades | `vidrio templado laminado fachada Asuncion`, `fachada vidrio aluminio Asuncion Paraguay`, `vidrio construccion Asuncion Paraguay`, `blindex Paraguay` |
   | Architecture | `arquitecto Asuncion Paraguay fachada`, `estudio arquitectura Asuncion` |
   | Development | `desarrolladora inmobiliaria Asuncion Paraguay`, `constructora edificio torre Asuncion` |
   | Hardware | `ferreteria construccion Asuncion`, `materiales construccion Asuncion` |
   | Aluminum | `carpinteria aluminio Asuncion`, `aberturas aluminio Asuncion` |

   **Pitfall**: Only ~7-8 results per query. To get more companies, run MORE
   queries (vary the search terms), not the same query repeatedly.

### Phase 3: Compilation & Deliverable

5. **Build the deliverable** as a git repo at `/root/<project-name>/`:

   ```
   project-name/
   ├── README.md
   ├── data/
   │   └── QUICK_REFERENCE.md     # Phone cheat sheet, tier lookup
   └── docs/
       ├── RESEARCH_SOURCES.md     # Methodology
       ├── 01_market_analysis/
       │   └── MARKET_ANALYSIS.md   # Market size, opportunities, trends
       ├── 02_customer_needs/
       │   └── CUSTOMER_NEEDS.md    # Per buyer type: pain points, value props
       ├── 03_competitors/
       │   └── COMPETITOR_ANALYSIS.md # Competitive landscape
       ├── 04_lead_database/
       │   └── LEAD_DATABASE.md     # Full leads with phone/email/address/tier
       └── 05_action_plan/
           └── ACTION_PLAN.md       # Prioritized outreach with call scripts
   ```

   **Tier system for leads**:
   - **Tier 1**: Direct buyers (companies that buy/install your product)
   - **Tier 2**: Channel partners (could resell or distribute)
   - **Tier 3**: Influencers (architects, engineers who specify)
   - **Tier 4**: End clients (developers, contractors who need the product)
   - **Tier 5**: Retail/distribution

   For each lead: name, website status (✅/❌), phone, email, address, rating,
   products/services, tier assignment, priority call order.

   Include a **phone cheat sheet** at the top of the lead database with all
   numbers sorted by priority.

   ```bash
   git init && git add -A && git commit -m "Initial commit: ..."
   ```

6. **Push to GitHub** under the Ai-Whisperers org:
   ```bash
   gh repo create Ai-Whisperers/<repo-name> --private --description "..."
   git remote add origin https://github.com/Ai-Whisperers/<repo-name>.git
   git push -u origin main
   ```

## Pitfalls & Lessons Learned

### Search Engine Blocks (This Will Happen)

- **Google**: CAPTCHA on every automated search from non-PY IPs
- **DuckDuckGo**: CAPTCHA + "select the duck" image challenges on lite.duckduckgo.com
- **Bing, Brave**: Same — all block
- **SearXNG**: Install locally if available; try `localhost:8888` or `localhost:4000`
- **Workaround**: Google Maps front-end via browser (not search API)

### Paraguayan Company Web Presence

- **80-90% of .com.py domains don't resolve** — this is normal
- Many companies operate via Facebook/Instagram/WhatsApp only
- DNS = NXDOMAIN does NOT mean the company doesn't exist
- Some DNS-resolved domains have SSL errors or timeout — still valid businesses

### Google Maps Pitfalls

- **Limited view**: only ~7-8 results per query, logged-in view has more
- **No emails**: emails rarely appear in the limited view — visit company website
- **Phone numbers are reliable** but may reach a retail storefront
- **Portuguese interface**: Maps defaults to Portuguese for Brazil-region IPs, but data is for Paraguay
- **Vision analysis fails**: browser_vision with image_url mode errors on some models. Rely on DOM snapshot.
- **LinkedIn blocks without login**: company pages show auth wall
- **News sites**: search results on ultimahora.com/abc.com.py return 10+ news stories, not structured data
- **Business directories** (Infobel, Cybo, Paginas Amarillas PY): all blocked by Cloudflare

### Repo & Deliverable Pitfalls

- **README.md should clarify what "client" means** — there can be confusion between the end customer and the repo's audience. Be explicit.
- **Paraguay specific**: phone numbers in Paraguay format (+595 XXX XXX XXX), addresses with "casi" (corner of), barrio names
- **Include a note about research limitations** — any blocked sources, data freshness, unverified phone numbers
- **The paragu-ai-leads repo exists** (beauty/wellness, 7,463 businesses) — check it early to avoid duplicating work, but note it's a different vertical
- **Landing page / mobile-first design**: most PY businesses browse on WhatsApp Web, not desktop

## Spanish/Portuguese Terms Reference

| English | Spanish | Portuguese (Maps UI) |
|---------|---------|---------------------|
| Glass shop | Vidrieria | Vidracaria |
| Hardware store | Ferreteria | Loja de ferramentas |
| Construction | Construccion | Construcao/Construtora |
| Facade | Fachada | Fachada |
| Curtain wall | Muro cortina | Muro cortina |
| Tempered glass | Vidrio templado | Vidro temperado |
| Laminated glass | Vidrio laminado | Vidro laminado |
| Developer | Desarrolladora | Desenvolvedora |
| Contractor | Constructora | Construtora |
| Rating (stars) | Estrellas | Estrelas |
| Website link | Sitio web | "Acessar o site de" |
| Open/Closed | Abierto/Cerrado | Aberto/Fechado |
| Phone | Telefono | Telefone |
| Route | Ruta | Rotas |
| Search | Buscar | Pesquisar |

## Post-Research: Website Planning (if user wants a site built)

After market research is complete, the user may ask to build a website for the
client. This is a **different class of site** from our B2C e-commerce template:

### B2B Lead Gen vs B2C E-commerce — Critical Differences

| Dimension | B2C (Fun4Me, Superspuma, depiflash) | B2B (construction glass, law firms) |
|-----------|--------------------------------------|--------------------------------------|
| Goal | Sell products online | Generate sales leads |
| Content | Product photos, prices | Technical specs, certifications, portfolio |
| Pricing | Display prices | "Request quote" — every project is custom |
| Trust signals | Reviews, ratings | Project portfolio, client logos, certs |
| CTA | "Buy now" / "Add to cart" | "Solicitar cotización" / WhatsApp |
| SEO keywords | Product names | "muro cortina Paraguay" (industry terms) |
| Decision cycle | Minutes | Weeks (projects are planned) |
| Theme | Dark, brand-forward | Clean, professional, industry-appropriate (blue for construction) |
| Feature priority | Cart, checkout, payments | Contact form, quote request, technical data |

### When the user asks "make a website for this client":

1. **Clarify**: Is this B2B or B2C? If B2B, the template-nextjs-client needs
   SIGNIFICANT modifications — don't just clone and change colors.

2. **Site architecture for B2B lead gen** (standard 7-page structure):
   ```
   /                 → Home (hero + product overview + trust signals + CTA)
   /nosotros         → About (company, team, certifications, numbers)
   /productos        → Products (technical specs WITHOUT prices, "request quote")
   /proyectos        → Portfolio (gallery of completed installations)
   /servicios        → Services (consulting, supply, installation, after-sales)
   /blog             → Blog (SEO content targeting zero-competition keywords)
   /contacto         → Contact (form + WhatsApp + phone + map)
   /cotizar          → Quote request (standalone form for campaign links)
   ```

3. **Content strategy** — every piece of content should answer:
   - What do you sell? (glass products with technical data)
   - Why should they trust you? (certifications, portfolio, experience)
   - How do they buy? (call or form, not cart)
   - What does it cost? (depends on project — request a quote)

4. **SEO advantage**: Paraguay's B2B market has ZERO companies doing SEO for
   industry keywords. A simple blog with 3-5 articles can dominate:
   - "muro cortina Paraguay"
   - "vidrio fachada Asunción"
   - "precio vidrio templado Paraguay"
   - "blindex Paraguay"

5. **Theme**: Use blue/white for construction (trust, professionalism, safety).
   Don't use the dark theme from the template — it looks wrong for B2B.

6. **No prices visible** — every project is custom. Use "Solicitar cotización"
   as the primary CTA. The contact/quote form replaces the cart.

## Verification Checklist

- [ ] Scope clarified (direct buyer vs channel vs influencer?)
- [ ] Wikipedia API scraped for relevant pages
- [ ] DNS batch check on 20+ domain variants
- [ ] Working company websites visited for contact info
- [ ] Google Maps searched with 3-5 different queries
- [ ] All found companies documented with phone + rating
- [ ] For DNS-resolved domains: websites visited and contact info extracted
- [ ] Lead tiers assigned
- [ ] Deliverable structured as a git repo
- [ ] Phone cheat sheet included
- [ ] Research sources / limitations documented
- [ ] Repo pushed to Ai-Whisperers GitHub org
- [ ] If user also wants a site: determined if B2B or B2C (they're built differently; see `references/b2b-construction-website-plan.md`)

messages.content: {"success": true, "name": "restaurant-website-nextjs", "description": "Build, deploy, and operate a single-locale restaurant or food-business website using Next.js 16 App Router + Tailwind v4. Includes menu/carta page with full categorized dishes, pricing, sushi combos, WhatsApp order flow (multi-item cart), gallery with real images, contact/map, and about page. No CMS, no database, no i18n. Content lives in content/es.json.", "tags": ["nextjs", "restaurant", "food", "menu", "tailwind-v4", "docker-swarm", "nextjs-16", "standalone", "whatsapp", "single-locale", "order-cart", "images"], "related_skills": ["professional-services-website", "event-website-sveltekit", "nextjs-docker-swarm"], "content": "--- name: restaurant-website-nextjs description: >- Build, deploy, and operate a single-locale restaurant or food-business website using Next.js 16 App Router + Tailwind v4. Includes menu/carta page with full categorized dishes, pricing, sushi combos, WhatsApp order flow (multi-item cart), gallery with real images, contact/map, and about page. No CMS, no database, no i18n. Content lives in content/es.json. tags: - nextjs - restaurant - food - menu - tailwind-v4 - docker-swarm - nextjs-16 - standalone - whatsapp - single-locale - order-cart - images triggers: - restaurant website - food business site - build a menu site - carta digital - menu page - food delivery site - restaurant landing page - sushi restaurant - chinese restaurant - menu online - online ordering restaurant - restaurant order page - interactive cart restaurant - multi-item order whatsapp - restaurant with images - unsplash restaurant images related_skills: - professional-services-website - event-website-sveltekit - nextjs-docker-swarm references: - tsuki-restaurante-build-session.md - tsuki-order-cart-and-images.md - tsuki-order-enhancements-seo-chatbot.md scripts: [] --- # Restaurant Website (Next.js 16 App Router + Tailwind v4) Build a single-locale restaurant website with a full online menu (carta), WhatsApp order flow (multi-item cart), gallery, contact/map, and about page. No CMS, no database, no i18n. Content lives in a single `content/es.json`. **Reference build:** `references/tsuki-restaurante-build-session.md` — full Tsuki Restaurante Oriental site (Chinese-sushi fusion in San Lorenzo). ## Architecture | Layer | Choice | |-------|--------| | Framework | Next.js 16 (App Router) | | Styling | Tailwind CSS v4 (`@tailwindcss/postcss`) | | Rendering | Static SSG (prerendered) | | Order Flow | Multi-item cart → WhatsApp deep link (client-side `useState` cart + `useMemo` total) | | Deploy | Docker (`node:20-alpine` standalone) on Swarm behind Traefik | | Content | JSON file (`content/es.json`) | | Lead capture | WhatsApp deep link with pre-formatted message | | Fonts | Inter (body) + Playfair Display (headings) | | Images | Downloaded from Unsplash for hero, gallery, features | ### Stack Versions (May 2026) ```json { "dependencies": { "lucide-react": "^0.510.0", "next": "^16.2.6", "react": "^19.2.6", "react-dom": "^19.2.6" }, "devDependencies": { "@tailwindcss/postcss": "^4.2.4", "tailwindcss": "^4.2.4", "typescript": "^5.8.0" } } ``` ## Project Structure ``` tsuki-restaurante/ ├── app/ │ ├── globals.css # @theme colors, component classes │ ├── layout.tsx # Root layout: fonts, Header, Footer, WhatsAppFloat │ ├── page.tsx # Homepage: Hero, Features, MenuPreview, Hours, CTA │ ├── robots.ts # SEO │ ├── sitemap.ts # SEO │ ├── carta/ │ │ ├── page.tsx # Server component (loads content, passes to client) │ │ └── menu-client.tsx # Client: category nav, table/list, accordion │ ├── pedidos/ │ │ ├── page.tsx # Server: loads content + menu categories │ │ └── order-client.tsx # Client: full interactive cart → WhatsApp │ ├── nosotros/ │ │ └── page.tsx # Server: story + values │ ├── contacto/ │ │ └── page.tsx # Server: info cards + Google Maps embed + WhatsApp CTA │ └── galeria/ │ ├── page.tsx # Server shell │ └── gallery-client.tsx # Client: filter buttons + masonry grid + lightbox ├── components/ │ ├── header.tsx # Fixed top nav, scroll-aware glass effect │ ├── footer.tsx # 3-column: brand, links, contact │ ├── hero.tsx # Full-screen hero with CTA buttons │ ├── features-section.tsx # 4-column icon grid │ ├── menu-preview-section.tsx # Category cards linking to /carta#category │ ├── cta-banner.tsx # Gradient CTA banner │ └── whatsapp-float.tsx # Fixed bottom-right WhatsApp button ├── content/ │ └── es.json # ALL site content: site, nav, home, menu, order, about, gallery, contact, footer ├── public/ │ └── images/ # Hero, gallery, og-image (downloaded from Unsplash) ├── next.config.mjs # output: 'standalone' ├── postcss.config.mjs # @tailwindcss/postcss ├── tsconfig.json ├── package.json ├── Dockerfile # Two-stage build, node:20-alpine └── docker-compose.yml # Swarm + Traefik labels + healthcheck ``` - Search/filter for order page - QR page for physical menu - Order API endpoint (`/api/order`) for server-side logging - Delivery details form (delivery method, payment method, observations) - JSON-LD structured data (Restaurant + LocalBusiness schema) - Full SEO: keywords, Google verification, Twitter Cards, canonical URLs - Full weekly hours (not just single day) ## Content Structure (`content/es.json`) | Section | Purpose | |---------|---------| | `site` | Name, phone, whatsapp, address, hours | | `navigation` | Array of `{href, label}` for nav links | | `home` | Hero content, features, menuSection, hours, cta | | `menu` | Full menu data — categories, items, prices, notes | | `order` | SEO, hero, categories for navigation, CTA text for cart | | `about` | Story paragraphs, values array | | `gallery` | Image array with src/alt/category | | `contact` | Info cards, map embed, WhatsApp CTA | | `footer` | Copyright, links, powered by | ### Menu Data Format Two item types: **List items** (most categories): ```json { "id": "entradas", "name": "Entradas", "items": [ { "name": "Arrolladitos de Primavera", "description": "Masa crocante...", "price": "Gs 20.000 (3 unid.) / Gs 30.000 (5 unid.)" } ], "notes": ["Apanado y frito a elección: +Gs 3.000"] } ``` **Table items** (e.g. Salteados Wok — protein × format matrix): ```json { "id": "salteados", "name": "Salteados Wok", "type": "table", "columns": ["Proteína", "Arroz Chino", "Fideo Arroz", "Chop Suey", "Yakisoba"], "rows": [ ["Pollo", "Gs 37.000", "Gs 37.000", "Gs 35.000", "Gs 38.000"] ] } ``` ## Color Palette Dark restaurant theme with gold/red accents: ```css @theme { --color-primary: #8B0000; --color-primary-light: #CD5C5C; --color-accent: #D4AF37; --color-background: #0D0D0D; --color-surface: #1A1A1A; --color-surface-alt: #222222; --color-text: #F5F0E8; --color-text-muted: #888080; } ``` ## Image Sourcing from Unsplash For a restaurant site, source free high-quality food images via direct Unsplash download URLs. Use `curl -sL -o ''` for each image. ### Unsplash direct download URL pattern ``` https://images.unsplash.com/photo-?w=&q=80 ``` No API key needed. The `w` param controls width. ### Good search queries for restaurant images - Chinese/interior: `Chinese restaurant interior dim sum` - Sushi: `sushi platter close up`, `japanese sushi rolls` - Wok/stir-fry: `chinese food stir fry wok`, `noodles chopsticks asian food` - Appetizers: `spring rolls vietnamese appetizer`, `gyoza dumplings japanese` - Seafood: `fried shrimp seafood asian` - Restaurant interior: `restaurant interior cozy` ### Download all images ```python food_images = { "hero-bg.jpg": {"url": "https://images.unsplash.com/photo-[REDACTED_PHONE]f8b828add9?w=1600&q=80"}, "sushi-1.jpg": {"url": "https://images.unsplash.com/photo-[REDACTED_PHONE]cf80d66c?w=800&q=80"}, "wok-1.jpg": {"url": "https://images.unsplash.com/photo-[REDACTED_PHONE]be2db19?w=800&q=80"}, } for filename, info in food_images.items(): terminal(f"curl -sL -o public/images/{filename} '{info['url']}'") ``` ### Files to generate Place images in `public/images/`: - `hero-bg.jpg` — 1600px wide for hero background - `sushi-1.jpg`, `sushi-2.jpg`, `wok-1.jpg`, `wok-2.jpg`, `spring-rolls.jpg`, `gyozas.jpg`, `shrimp.jpg`, `restaurant-interior.jpg` — 800px for gallery/features Update `content/es.json` to reference these paths in both `gallery.images[]` and `home.hero.bgImage`. ## Order Page (`/pedidos`) — Interactive Cart → WhatsApp The order page is a client component (`"use client"`) with full cart state management. It reuses the same menu `categories` data structure as the carta page, but each item has an "add to cart" button instead of just displaying. ### Architecture ``` Server page (pedidos/page.tsx) → loads content/es.json (menu.categories, site.whatsapp, order.cta) → passes data as props to client component → OrderPageClient (pedidos/order-client.tsx) → STATE: cart (CartItem[]), activeCat, openCat, showCart, name, sent → Left: menu + "+" buttons per item → Right/overlay: cart sidebar with qty controls + send button ``` ### Cart Data Model ```typescript interface CartItem { id: string; // `${name}::${price}` — dedup key name: string; qty: number; price: string; // "Gs 20.000" — original price string } ``` ### Cart Logic ```typescript const [cart, setCart] = useState([]); // Add item (increment if exists, else push) function addItem(name: string, price: string) { const id = `${name}::${price}`; setCart((prev) => { const existing = prev.find((i) => i.id === id); if (existing) return prev.map((i) => i.id === id ? {...i, qty: i.qty + 1} : i); return [...prev, {id, name, qty: 1, price}]; }); } // Update quantity or remove at 0 function updateQty(id: string, delta: number) { setCart((prev) => prev.map((i) => i.id === id ? {...i, qty: Math.max(0, i.qty + delta)} : i).filter((i) => i.qty > 0)); } // Auto-calculate total const cartTotal = useMemo(() => cart.reduce((sum, item) => sum + parsePrice(item.price) * item.qty, 0), [cart]); ``` ### WhatsApp Message Format The message opens WhatsApp with a pre-formatted order. For enhanced orders with delivery details, the format expands: ``` 🧾 Pedido Tsuki Restaurante Oriental • Arrolladitos de Primavera x2 — Gs 20.000 • New York Roll x1 — Gs 27.000 Total: Gs 139.000 ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ *Nombre:* Juan Pérez *Modalidad:* Retiro en local *Pago:* Efectivo *Observaciones:* Sin cebolla ``` ### Send Flow ```typescript function handleSend() { // 1. Open WhatsApp with order window.open(`https://wa.me/${whatsapp}?text=${msg}`, "_blank"); // 2. Fire-and-forget POST to API route for server-side logging fetch('/api/order', { method: 'POST', body: JSON.stringify({ items, total, name, deliveryMethod, deliveryAddress, paymentMethod, observation, timestamp }) }).catch(() => {}); // silent fail — don't block WhatsApp } ``` ### UI Layout | Viewport | Layout | |----------|--------| | Desktop (lg+) | Two-column: menu on left (flex-1) + sticky cart sidebar (w-80) | | Mobile | Menu full-width + FAB cart button (fixed bottom-20) + bottom sheet drawer | ### Cart Features - **Per-item qty controls**: +/- buttons, remove (trash icon) - **Item dedup**: Same item+price = increment qty, not duplicate row - **Auto total**: `useMemo` recalculates when cart changes - **Name field**: Optional text input included in the message - **Delivery details form**: Radio buttons for delivery method (Retiro en local / Delivery), conditional address input, radio for payment method (Efectivo / Transferencia), textarea for observations - **Search/filter**: Search input with 300ms debounce, filters item names case-insensitively across all categories, shows "No encontramos platos" when no results - **Send button**: Opens WhatsApp with pre-formatted message including all order details - **Clear all**: Single button to empty cart - **Confirmation state**: Button shows checkmark briefly after send - **Desktop sidebar**: Sticky, always visible - **Mobile FAB**: Floating action button with count badge → bottom sheet drawer ### Order Page CTA Content (in content/es.json) ```json "order": { "seo": { "title": "Pedidos — Tsuki Restaurante Oriental", "description": "..." }, "hero": { "title": "Hacé tu pedido", "subtitle": "..." }, "categories": [{ "id": "entradas", "name": "Entradas" }, ...], "search": { "placeholder": "Buscar plato..." }, "cta": { "title": "Confirmá tu pedido", "subtitle": "...", "buttonText": "Enviar pedido por WhatsApp", "emptyText": "Tu carrito está vacío — agregá productos de la carta", "detailsTitle": "Detalles del pedido", "deliveryLabel": "Modalidad", "deliveryLocal": "Retiro en local", "deliveryAddress": "Delivery", "addressPlaceholder": "Dirección de entrega", "paymentLabel": "Método de pago", "paymentCash": "Efectivo", "paymentTransfer": "Transferencia", "observationLabel": "Observaciones", "observationPlaceholder": "Ej: sin cebolla, bien cocido...", "nameLabel": "Nombre", "namePlaceholder": "Tu nombre" } } ``` ## QR Page (`/qr`) — Printable Menu QR A server component that generates a real scannable QR code pointing to `/pedidos`. Uses the `qrcode` npm package: ```tsx import QRCode from "qrcode"; async function generateQrSvg(url: string): Promise { return QRCode.toString(url, { type: "svg", margin: 2, width: 300, color: { dark: "#1a1a1a", light: "#ffffff" }, }); } ``` Key features: - White background, restaurant name, QR, instructions - `@media print` styles hide header/footer, enlarge QR - URL shown at bottom in monospace - Disallowed from search indexing (`robots: { index: false, follow: false }`) ## Order API (`/api/order`) A Next.js App Router API route for server-side order logging: ```tsx // app/api/order/route.ts export async function POST(request: Request) { const body = await request.json(); // Validate body.items exists // Append to /tmp/tsuki-orders.log as JSON line // Optionally try Evolution API if key is available return Response.json({ success: true }); } ``` The order page fires a fire-and-forget `fetch POST /api/order` after opening WhatsApp.** ## SEO & Structured Data ### JSON-LD Schema.org Add to `app/layout.tsx` inside the body tag: ```tsx

{"success": true, "name": "self-hosted-meeting-infrastructure", "description": "Deploy and operate self-hosted meeting transcription infrastructure — Vexa meeting bots, Speaches (Whisper ASR), MinIO storage, and the delivery pipeline for recordings + transcripts + summaries. Covers Docker networking, Traefik integration alongside existing Swarm services, and Telegram notification wiring.", "tags": [], "related_skills": [], "content": "---
